channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S. payrolls rose by 172,000 in May, much more than expected; unemployment at 4.3%
item.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to increase by 80,000 in May while the unemployment rate held at 4.3%.

item.title: Alphabet is seeking fresh capital as stock's 4-week losing streak tests investor appetite
item.description: Alphabet expects capex to reach up to $190 billion this year, double last year's spending, and the company is turning to investors to help fund its expansion.

item.title: Bitcoin cracks $60,000, sinking to lowest level since October 2024
item.description: The weeklong sell-off was exacerbated after a stronger-than-expected May jobs report Friday sent yields higher and pressured risk assets.

item.title: U.S. Senate passes $70 billion ICE funding, fails to ban Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund
item.description: Nearly all of the funding would go to DHS's ICE and Border Patrol agencies that are carrying out the Trump admin's vigorous deportations throughout the U.S.

item.title: Broadcom's guidance sent the stock tumbling this week. A buying opportunity has emerged
item.description: Jeff Kilburg breaks down how he's trading Broadcom after this week's declines.

item.title: Boeing to start 737 Max production on new assembly line July 6, CEO says
item.description: The new 737 Max final assembly line in Everett, Washington, will serve as a catalyst for increasing Max production to 52 jets per month.

item.title: Lululemon cuts annual outlook, citing 'negative' media commentary and disappointing product launches
item.description: Lululemon is expecting its situation to get a lot worse before it gets better, as it issued weak guidance for the full year.

item.title: The gold chart looks precarious. Here's how to profit
item.description: Gold is at a technically precarious juncture, and the good news for you is that options market may be mispricing the risk.

item.title: No tech, no problem. Dow makes record and traders think these stocks can lead now
item.description: Traders bought about 5,300 calls in the State Street Health Care Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLV) on Thursday, compared to just over 1,000 puts, according to data from ThinkOrSwim.

item.title: Odds of a Fed hike this year jump on prediction markets
item.description: Following an unexpectedly hot jobs report, prediction market traders see a 52% chance the Federal Reserve increases interest rates this year.

item.title: Warren Buffett disciple Guy Spier built career on value investing. A rare cancer changed everything
item.description: After a glioblastoma diagnosis, value investor Guy Spier rethought wealth, time and legacy — and the impact he can have on rare disease research.

item.title: Inside the $850 million Obama Presidential Center ahead of its Juneteenth opening
item.description: President Obama's library will cost $30 to visit, making it the most expensive presidential museum or library in modern history.

item.title: AI is designing OpenAI's next model in a sign of 'superintelligence': SoftBank's Masayoshi Son to CNBC
item.description: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son said his forecast of artificial superintelligence arriving in 10 years was "conservative" and thinks it will be here sooner.

item.title: JPMorgan removes sell rating on Tesla after years, says it's at the 'forefront of physical AI'
item.description: The firm has rated Elon Musk's electric vehicle company as sell dating back to 2023.

item.title: Amazon unveils latest warehouse robot as tech giants continue AI layoffs
item.description: "Our experience of robots is that it's actually driven up employment rather than the reverse," Amazon executive John Boumphrey told CNBC.

item.title: LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman is leaving Microsoft's board after almost a decade
item.description: Reid Hoffman was one of OpenAI's first donors, and he left its board in 2023 as the nonprofit cozied up with Microsoft.

item.title: Broadway's $1.9 billion season is the latest sign of consumers splurging on experiences
item.description: Sunday's Tony Awards follow a record-breaking season on Broadway. The awards often lead to further ticket sales for winning shows.

item.title: Trump Accounts don't 'rule' child investments, advisor says: How your options compare
item.description: Trump Accounts, along with 529 plans, custodial accounts for minors and Roth IRAs, are all valuable options depending on the child's needs and long-term goals.

item.title: Family offices bet on sports, from pickleball leagues to smart soccer balls
item.description: While AI startups dominate deal-making news, investment firms of the ultra-rich are still keen on sports.

item.title: Where investors may find the next 'big wave' for AI trade
item.description: Tim Urbanowicz, chief investment strategist at Innovator from Goldman Sachs Asset Management, tackles the AI boom.

item.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Friday
item.description: May jobs report came in hot, tech sell-off deepens, and Lululemon's troubles mount.

item.title: CNBC Select’s weekly mortgage rate snapshot: See your options today
item.description: Here's what mortgage rates are for the week ending June 4, according to Freddie Mac.

item.title: OpenAI says it will comply with Trump's order requiring AI model reviews before release
item.description: George Osborne, OpenAI's Head of Countries told CNBC that governments "have a big role to play in how this technology is used and deployed."

item.title: How the Strait of Hormuz standoff flipped the energy security debate
item.description: Iran's influence over the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz has inadvertently transformed the energy security debate.

item.title: The Tech Download: Anthropic’s IPO sets up first big test of AI boom valuations
item.description: Anthropic took a big step this week towards pipping bitter rival OpenAI to a public market listing.

item.title: Warren invites Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to Senate hearing on China AI chip sales
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren is pressing Jensen Huang's Nvidia over export controls, China sales, Trump and data-center policy as Congress scrutinizes the AI chip boom.

item.title: Trump says he could meet Iran's supreme leader 'if it was to make a deal'
item.description: Khamenei was named the supreme leader in Iran after his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed on the first day of fighting.

item.title: CrowdStrike CEO says AI security fears will become a bigger tailwind in coming quarters
item.description: CrowdStrike CEO George Kurtz said it was too early for concerns surrounding Anthropic's Mythos to meaningfully impact first-quarter results.

item.title: India's economy expands at 7.8% over January to March — faster than expected
item.description: The world's fastest growing major economy, India, has been facing major headwinds due to the conflict in Middle East that began end of February.

item.title: Ramp hits $44 billion valuation as companies look to rein in AI spending
item.description: Ramp's latest funding round was led by ICONIQ, GIC and the Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan.
